name: claude-parallel-accounts
description: Companion for the Claude Code VS Code extension: run a different Claude account in each VSCode window simultaneously — capture, switch, and isolate accounts per window.

virtualWorkspaces.description: Claude accounts live in data directories on the local file system, which virtual workspaces don't have.

untrustedWorkspaces.description: Works in Restricted Mode. The extension never reads or executes workspace content — it only uses the workspace folder path to tell windows apart. Until you trust the folder, its workspace settings cannot override this extension's configuration.

claudeProfiles.sharedHistory.markdownDescription:
Keep **one conversation history shared by all your accounts**.

Claude Code normally stores chats inside the account's own data directory, so switching account would make them vanish. With this on, history lives in one store (`~/.claude-shared`) that every account links to — hit a usage limit, switch account, and carry on in the same conversation.

Credentials and identity are never shared; they stay strictly per-account. Turning this off gives each account its own full copy of the history from that moment on.
